smell_name: Merge API Parameter Not Explicitly Set
description: A df.merge() call is missing one or more of the following parameters: on (or left_on/right_on), how, and validate. Without these, Pandas silently merges on all common column names, defaults to inner join which may drop rows, and does not validate key uniqueness, all of which can cause silent data corruption.
fix_strategy: Infer the correct values for on, how, and validate from the surrounding DataFrame definitions, variable names, and comments within the context window. If the correct value cannot be determined with confidence, apply a safe default and insert a # TODO comment flagging the inferred value for developer review.

canonical_examples.description: Simple merge missing all three parameters
canonical_examples.after:
result = df1.merge(
    df2,
    on='user_id',
    how='inner',
    validate='many_to_one'
)

canonical_examples.description: Merge with context suggesting left join and known key
canonical_examples.after:
sales = transactions.merge(
    customers,
    on='customer_id',
    how='left',
    validate='many_to_one'
)

canonical_examples.description: Merge where context is insufficient — safe defaults applied
canonical_examples.after:
result = df_a.merge(
    df_b,
    on=None,  # TODO: verify join key
    how='inner',
    validate='one_to_many'
)

false_positive_patterns.description: All three parameters are already explicitly specified.
Merge is on a single unambiguous index with left_on and right_on already specified.

context_window_hints: Look for column names defined in preceding pd.read_csv() or pd.DataFrame() calls
Look for comments mentioning join type (left, right, outer, inner)
Look for drop_duplicates() or duplicated() calls after the merge, which suggest one_to_one or many_to_one validation
Look for variable names that suggest the relationship (e.g. users, transactions suggests many_to_one)
If schemas are defined in external modules, apply safe defaults
verification_check: Verify that all three parameters — on (or left_on/right_on), how, and validate — are explicitly present in the refactored merge call.
